hard_stop_loss.definition: P&L <= 0 and exit tag contains Stop, excluding the earlier more-specific categories. Represents trades that made some progress but still reverted into a stop-based loss.
hard_stop_loss.theme: moderate progress reversal before durable protection
hard_stop_loss.next_step_posture: highest-priority unresolved weakness; if iteration continues, prefer a new mid-trade protection ratchet between BE activation and full profit-lock activation

method: Alpaca-backed local feasibility test for a structural ORB re-entry exit on top of v26. Once a breakout has made enough favourable progress, the branch exits if price is later accepted back inside the original ORB for a configurable number of consecutive bars.

structural_conclusion.research_interpretation: This branch is structurally different from timeout, giveback, low-progress, and mid-ratchet lines because it treats breakout failure as a re-acceptance back into the original ORB zone.
structural_conclusion.next_step_rule: Do not launch a QC candidate unless the branch improves full-sample Alpaca PnL and shows at least 2/4 improved time folds and 3+ improved years.
